Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Therapy Dog Visit & Story Time
events.description: Join us for a small group visit with a therapy dog. Children will have an opportunity to read, listen to stories and engage in therapeutic human-animal interaction. This program is presented in partnership with One Health Partners. Please note: A maximum of five children may participate at a time, with each session lasting ten minutes during the one-hour visit.

events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Use our guides to build a creation or use your creativity to build what you want!

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Summer Wonder Camp: Presented by Dino Bites
events.description: Come join us at the library for a 90 min workshop for 3 days! Handle and discuss real and replica fossils of dinosaurs and other extinct beasts from all over the world. Discover the wild world of dinosaurs with an expert and learn about fossils evolution and the ‘Tree of Life.' Presented by Dino Bites. Ages 4-12.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  March Break Challenge: Build a mall as a group from 2-3pm If you identify as a person with a disability and require an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email, accessibleservices@tpl.ca, or voicemail, 416-393-7099, to make a request. Please contact us at le

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: STEM Programming For Children
events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Pokémon Club
events.description: Whether you're a new or expert Pokémon Trainer, everyone is welcome! Show off your Pokémon knowledge and research skills and join our exciting Pokémon scavenger hunt for free Pokémon cards! Feel free to bring in your old Pokémon cards to play with or trade with staff and other Pokémon trainers!

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: This month we will play with electricity and circuitry. Turn on a light, spin a motor, activate a speaker and choose from hundreds of different projects, or try making your own circuits! For ages 6-12. Registration is required. Parent/Caregiver must attend. events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis.  Suitable for ages 6-12. Dash: May 3, June 7, July 5, August 9 LEGO: May 10, June 14, July 12, August 16 Snap Circuits: May 24, June 21, July 19, August 23 Sphero: May 31, June 28, July 26, August 30

events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.title: History of Generative Music: The Last 50 Years
events.description: From the first computer-generated compositions to the AI music tools available today, the past five decades have seen generative music evolve at an extraordinary pace. events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, wher

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Youth Hub: Beaded Friendship Bracelets
events.description: Create your own unique friendship bracelets to share with your friends. Perfect for showing off your creativity and making lasting memories! All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, wher

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service.
